title: Ancient Cities
description: Major biblical and historical cities used for orientation in the 3D Journey panel.

features.name: Jerusalem
features.description: The central city of worship, kingship, and later temple-centered life.
features.caution: The site is secure, but the precise footprint of older city phases changed over time.

features.name: Jericho
features.description: An oasis city near the Jordan valley and a recurring waypoint in biblical narrative.
features.caution: The city is well located, though the exact ancient mound level varies by period.

features.name: Bethlehem
features.description: A small Judean town associated with David and later the birth narratives of Jesus.
features.caution: The location is secure, but the ancient settlement extent is approximate.

features.name: Nazareth
features.description: The Galilean town associated with Jesus' upbringing.
features.caution: The settlement is well known from the New Testament period, though first-century extent is modest.

features.name: Capernaum
features.description: A major base for Jesus' Galilean ministry on the north shore of the Sea of Galilee.
features.caution: The archaeological site is clear, but the Gospel narratives compress repeated visits.

features.name: Shechem
features.description: An important central highland city linked with covenant scenes and later national gatherings.
features.caution: The biblical city is secure, though the exact ancient tell and nearby settlements shifted over time.

features.name: Hebron
features.description: A southern hill country city associated with the patriarchs and David's early reign.
features.caution: The general location is secure, but the ancient urban footprint is layered.

features.name: Damascus
features.description: A major northern city and trade hub that appears frequently in prophetic and apostolic contexts.
features.caution: The city is secure, though its ancient center evolved across many imperial periods.

features.name: Babylon
features.description: The imperial city tied to exile, court life, and later restoration memory.
features.caution: The city is historically secure, but the biblical setting often refers to the wider imperial center rather than one district.

features.name: Rome
features.description: The imperial capital that frames the later New Testament world.
features.caution: The city is certain; the local locations in Paul's Roman stay are more reconstructed.
